questions.question: Why do MNCs prefer to invest in countries with Special Economic Zones (SEZs)?
questions.explanation: SEZs offer tax incentives and improved infrastructure to attract foreign investment.

questions.question: How can globalisation increase income inequality?
questions.explanation: Large companies often benefit more than small producers, increasing inequality.

questions.question: Which situation best reflects the impact of liberalisation on Indian markets?

options.B: Increase in imported goods and competition

questions.explanation: Liberalisation allowed more imports and increased competition.

questions.question: Why do developed countries influence WTO decisions?

options.B: They have strong economic and political power

questions.explanation: Developed nations have greater influence due to economic strength.

questions.question: Which example shows integration of markets through globalisation?

options.A: Local sale of handmade goods only
options.B: Indian IT company serving US clients

questions.explanation: Providing services across borders shows global integration.

questions.question: How does reduction of tariffs affect domestic producers?
questions.explanation: Lower tariffs make foreign goods cheaper, increasing competition.

questions.question: Which group benefits most from globalisation in developing countries?
questions.explanation: Large firms and skilled workers gain more opportunities.

questions.question: Why is outsourcing important for developing countries?

options.B: It creates job opportunities in services

questions.explanation: Outsourcing generates employment in IT and service sectors.

questions.question: Which factor attracts MNCs to India?

options.B: Large consumer market and skilled labor

questions.explanation: India’s market size and skilled workforce attract MNCs.

questions.question: How can globalisation negatively affect local industries?

options.B: By exposing them to tough foreign competition

questions.explanation: Small firms may not survive against global competition.

questions.question: Which reform helped Indian producers access global markets?
questions.explanation: Economic reforms opened Indian markets globally.

questions.question: Why is global supply chain important?

options.B: It allows production stages in different countries

questions.explanation: Different production stages occur in various countries.

questions.question: Which action can protect workers from negative impacts of globalisation?
questions.explanation: Labor laws protect workers’ rights.

questions.question: Why do consumers gain from increased competition?
questions.explanation: Competition improves quality and reduces prices.

questions.question: Which scenario shows uneven benefits of globalisation?

options.B: Large companies expand while small producers close

questions.explanation: Small producers may suffer due to competition.

questions.question: How does technology strengthen globalisation?

options.B: Faster transfer of information and funds

questions.explanation: Technology enables quick communication and transactions.

questions.question: Which policy reduces foreign competition?
questions.explanation: Higher tariffs discourage imports.

questions.question: Which of the following reflects economic interdependence?

options.B: Country imports raw materials and exports finished goods

questions.explanation: Countries depend on each other for goods and services.

questions.question: Why do MNCs invest in infrastructure development?
questions.explanation: Infrastructure supports efficient operations.

questions.question: Which measure ensures fair global trade?
questions.explanation: Transparent policies ensure fairness.

questions.question: Which sector in India faced challenges due to cheap imports?
questions.explanation: Small-scale industries struggled against cheap imports.

questions.question: Which reform encouraged foreign investment in India?
questions.explanation: 1991 reforms encouraged FDI.

questions.question: How can governments ensure inclusive globalisation?

options.B: Providing support and training to small businesses

questions.explanation: Support helps small producers compete.

questions.question: Which example shows fair trade practice?
questions.explanation: Fair wages protect workers in global markets.

questions.question: Balanced globalisation requires:
questions.explanation: Globalisation should promote growth along with fairness.
